title: Form W-9: Request for Taxpayer Identification Number and Certification
content: Form W-9 is used by U.S. persons (including resident aliens) to provide their correct taxpayer identification number (TIN) to the person who is required to file an information return with the IRS. The form is commonly requested by banks, financial institutions, and businesses before they issue payments to independent contractors, freelancers, or vendors. The requester uses the TIN to report income paid to the IRS on forms such as 1099-MISC or 1099-NEC. The W-9 is not filed with the IRS directly — it is retained by the requesting party.
